name: Power of Attorney
description: A document authorizing one person to act on behalf of another in legal or financial matters.

sections.title: Declaration
sections.content:
I, {{principal_name}}, residing at {{principal_address}} ("Principal"), hereby appoint {{agent_name}}, residing at {{agent_address}} ("Agent" or "Attorney-in-Fact"), to act on my behalf as my true and lawful attorney-in-fact, effective as of {{effective_date}}.

This is a {{poa_type}} Power of Attorney.

sections.title: Grant of Authority
sections.content:
I grant my Agent the following powers and authority to act on my behalf:

(a) Financial matters: To manage, buy, sell, or otherwise deal with my real and personal property; to conduct banking transactions; to manage investments; to file tax returns and interact with taxing authorities; to manage retirement accounts and benefits.

(b) Legal matters: To engage and dismiss attorneys, accountants, and other professionals; to execute legal documents on my behalf; to commence, prosecute, defend, or settle legal proceedings.

(c) Personal matters: To make decisions regarding my personal affairs as may be necessary and appropriate.

(d) Business matters: To conduct business on my behalf, including entering into contracts, managing business interests, and making business decisions.

sections.title: Scope Limitations
Durability
sections.content: If this is a durable Power of Attorney, this instrument shall not be affected by my subsequent disability or incapacity. If this is not a durable Power of Attorney, this instrument shall become void upon my disability or incapacity.

sections.title: Alternate Agent
sections.content: If the Agent named above is unable or unwilling to serve, I appoint {{alternate_agent_name}} as my alternate Agent, with all the same powers and authority granted to the primary Agent herein.

sections.title: Agent's Duties
sections.content: The Agent shall: (a) act in the Principal's best interest; (b) act within the scope of authority granted; (c) keep accurate records of all transactions conducted on behalf of the Principal; (d) keep the Principal's property separate from the Agent's personal property; and (e) act with the care, competence, and diligence ordinarily exercised by agents in similar circumstances.

sections.title: Compensation and Reimbursement
sections.content: The Agent shall be entitled to reasonable compensation for services rendered under this Power of Attorney, unless otherwise agreed. The Agent shall be entitled to reimbursement for all reasonable expenses incurred in connection with the performance of duties under this Power of Attorney.

sections.title: Termination
sections.content: This Power of Attorney shall terminate upon: (a) the Principal's death; (b) the Principal's written revocation delivered to the Agent; (c) the expiration date specified herein, if any; (d) the Agent's death, incapacity, or resignation; or (e) a court order revoking the Power of Attorney.

sections.title: Third-Party Reliance
sections.content: Any third party who receives a copy of this Power of Attorney may rely upon the authority granted herein unless such third party has received actual notice of revocation or termination. No third party shall be liable for acting in good faith reliance on this Power of Attorney.

sections.title: Governing Law
sections.content: This Power of Attorney shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of {{governing_law_state}}.

sections.title: Signatures
sections.content:
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have executed this Power of Attorney as of the date first written above.

Principal: {{principal_name}}
Signature: ___________________________
Date: ___________________________

Witness 1:
Name: ___________________________
Signature: ___________________________
Date: ___________________________

Witness 2:
Name: ___________________________
Signature: ___________________________
Date: ___________________________

NOTARY ACKNOWLEDGMENT
State of {{governing_law_state}}
County of ___________________________

On this _____ day of _______________, 20___, before me, the undersigned notary public, personally appeared {{principal_name}}, proved to me through satisfactory evidence of identification to be the person whose name is signed on the preceding document, and acknowledged to me that they signed it voluntarily for its stated purpose.

Notary Public: ___________________________
My Commission Expires: ___________________________
